item.title: And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable…
item.description:
And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable Sir, a bhikkhu with taints destroyed lives respecting and revering the Teacher; lives respecting and revering the Dhamma; lives respecting and revering the Saṅgha; lives respecting and revering the training; lives respecting and revering concentration. This, Venerable Sir, is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching." - "Good, good, Sāriputta! This is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 48.58
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Householder, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in…
item.description:
- Householder, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in those forms, praises them, and abides with attachment to them, his consciousness becomes dependent on that and clings to it. Householder, with clinging, that bhikkhu does not attain complete peace... Householder, there are tastes cognizable by the tongue... Householder, there are mind-objects cognizable by the mind that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in those mind-objects, praises them, and abides with attachment to them, his consciousness becomes dependent on that and clings to it. With clinging, householder, that bhikkhu does not attain Nibbāna. This, householder, is the cause, this is the condition why some beings here and now do not attain complete peace.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 35.124
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Then Māra approached the Blessed One; having approached, he spoke this verse to the Blessed One: With a sorrowful mind, You meditate in the forest,…
item.description:
Then Māra approached the Blessed One; having approached, he spoke this verse to the Blessed One:
With a sorrowful mind,
You meditate in the forest,
Because your wealth has wasted away,
Or because you long for wealth?
Perhaps you have committed a crime in the village.
Why do you not make friends
With your relatives and neighbors?
Why can you not
Make friends with anyone?

( Blessed One ):
All roots of sorrow pulled out,
Without crime, I meditate,
Without sorrow, I meditate.
All craving for existence destroyed,
Taint-free, I meditate,
O heedless relative!

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 4.24
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Among the attached, he is unattached; Among the conceited, he is not conceited; He has understood suffering, both field and ground — The Tathāgata is…
item.description:
Among the attached, he is unattached;
Among the conceited, he is not conceited;
He has understood suffering, both field and ground —
The Tathāgata is worthy of the sacrificial offering.

Not dependent on longings, he sees seclusion,
Beyond the knowledge and views of others,
Having no object of reliance —
The Tathāgata is worthy of the sacrificial offering.

He has realized the Dhamma, far and near,
Completely destroyed, no longer existing,
Peaceful, without clinging, fully liberated —
The Tathāgata is worthy of the sacrificial offering.

From: Sutta Nipāta 3.4
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, if wanderers of other sects should ask you: 'For what purpose, friends, is the holy life lived under Samaṇa Gotama?' Being asked thus, bhikkhus,…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, if wanderers of other sects should ask you: 'For what purpose, friends, is the holy life lived under Samaṇa Gotama?' Being asked thus, bhikkhus, you should answer those wanderers: 'For the purpose of the destruction of greed, friends, the holy life is lived under Samaṇa Gotama.' Bhikkhus, if then those wanderers ask you: 'Is there a path, is there a way, friends, leading to the destruction of greed?' Being asked thus, bhikkhus, you should answer those wanderers: 'There is a path, there is a way leading to the destruction of greed.' And what, bhikkhus, is that path, that way? It is this Noble Eightfold Path, that is, right view ... right concentration. Bhikkhus, this is the path, this is the way leading to the destruction of greed. Being asked thus, bhikkhus, you should answer those wanderers in this way.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 45.41
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Mahā Kassapa (continued): Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock, Sits in meditation, without clinging, free from fear and terror. Returning from alms…
item.description:
Elder Mahā Kassapa (continued):

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, free from fear and terror.

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, burned, cooled.

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, duty done, without taints.

Enough for me, mindful, ardent, desiring to meditate.
Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, desiring benefit.

Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, desiring happiness.
Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, practicing yoga.

From: Theragāthā 18.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Great King, even wealthy nobles, wealthy brahmins, wealthy householders, rich, with great wealth and property, with abundant gold and silver, abundant goods and grains…
item.description:
- Great King, even wealthy nobles, wealthy brahmins, wealthy householders, rich, with great wealth and property, with abundant gold and silver, abundant goods and grains — it is for the sake of sensual pleasures, because of sensual pleasures, on account of sensual pleasures, that they consciously speak falsehood. Therefore, they will undergo long-term harm and suffering.

People are intoxicated
By wealth and by sensual desires,
They are greedy, deluded
In the sensual pleasures of the world,
Not clearly aware,
Excessively infatuated,
Like a fish that does not see the trap set,
Later they suffer,
Experiencing the result of evil deeds.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 3.7
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Then the Venerable Samiddhi, knowing it was Māra, spoke this verse to Māra: With faith, I left home to homelessness, My mindfulness and wisdom grow,…
item.description:
Then the Venerable Samiddhi, knowing it was Māra, spoke this verse to Māra:
With faith,
I left home to homelessness,
My mindfulness and wisdom grow,
My mind is concentrated.
Whatever form you create,
It does not frighten me.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 4.22
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The accumulation of bones from a single eon Would be a mountain as high, So said the Great Teacher. That mountain is said to be…
item.description:
The accumulation of bones from a single eon
Would be a mountain as high,
So said the Great Teacher.

That mountain is said to be
As great as Mount Vepulla,
North of Vulture's Peak
In the Magadhan hills.

But one who sees the Four Noble Truths
With perfect wisdom,
Suffering and its origin,
Will transcend all suffering;
The Noble Eightfold Path
Leads to suffering's stilling.

That person will wander on
At most seven more lives.
He has made an end of suffering,
Destroyed all fetters.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 15.10
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, I do not see any other class of beings as diverse as the mind. Even the animals, bhikkhus, are diverse because of the mind.…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, I do not see any other class of beings as diverse as the mind. Even the animals, bhikkhus, are diverse because of the mind. Therefore, bhikkhus, the mind is even more diverse than those animals. Therefore, bhikkhus, one should always contemplate one's own mind thus: 'For a long time, this mind has been defiled by greed, hatred, and delusion. Through the mind's defilement, beings are defiled; through the mind's purification, beings are purified.'

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 22.100
📖 Read this sutta
